V.reason: The workflow completed a real Codex contract smoke and produced a validated offline evidence ledger, but it has not yet demonstrated paired outcome lift.

U.reason: It uniquely reconciles method evidence into per-axis, per-platform, and explicit combination records while keeping evaluator-local scores separate.

E.reason: Eighteen deterministic tests and a real Promptfoo contract run exercised evidence gates, artifact verification, and orchestration semantics.

X.reason: The score model represents five harnesses, but the installed workflow and this run are Codex-native; no Claude Code, Pi, Hermes, or OpenCode adapter ran.

R.reason: The implementation passed deterministic tests, fail-closed proof checks, two independent final reviews, and one real contract smoke.

S.reason: Validated paths, symlink defenses, bounded files, digest pinning, secret-minimized subprocess environments, CSP, and read-only Promptfoo execution are implemented and tested.

M.reason: The project has no runtime dependencies and one deterministic core, but the validator-renderer script and comprehensive specification remain sizable.

F.reason: The personal Codex plugin installed and enabled successfully, its cache matched source, and the Codex app-server smoke passed.

C.reason: Plugin Eval measured a 134-token trigger and 2,014-token invocation, both moderate; its heavy deferred estimate includes repository files that are not always loaded.

methods.name: source-review
methods.exactCommand: /usr/bin/time -p sh -c 'npm test && npm run check && npm audit --omit=dev'

metrics.purpose: read-only contract smoke, not paired outcome measurement

codex.adapter: Promptfoo openai:codex-app-server:gpt-5.4 plus installed Codex plugin

V.reason: The Codex contract smoke passed, but outcome lift is not paired.

U.reason: The orchestration and ledger combination is differentiated on Codex.

E.reason: The contract and evidence gates were directly exercised.

X.reason: The data model is portable, but this adapter is Codex-specific.

R.reason: Installation, tests, and the Codex smoke passed.

S.reason: The Codex run was read-only and proof/path defenses passed tests.

M.reason: Dependency-free, with one sizable core script.

F.reason: The plugin is installed, enabled, hash-matched, and smoke-tested in Codex.

C.reason: Trigger and invocation budgets were moderate.

claude-code.reason: Version 0.1 is packaged as a Codex plugin and no Claude Code adapter ran.

pi.reason: No Pi installation or behavioral adapter exists in version 0.1.

hermes.reason: No Hermes installation or behavioral adapter exists in version 0.1.

opencode.reason: No OpenCode installation or behavioral adapter exists in version 0.1.

findings: The final Promptfoo contract smoke passed 1 of 1 trials with deterministic structural and JavaScript assertions.
Plugin Eval scored 67/D, primarily because a private local plugin intentionally lacks public privacy and terms URLs; this method score is not the aggregate score.
All 18 deterministic tests, audit, core review, and browser review passed with no remaining required findings.
Cross-platform and paired outcome value remain unmeasured.
unresolvedFacts: Outcome lift versus native Codex behavior has not been measured with paired repeated tasks.
Claude Code, Pi, Hermes, and OpenCode adapters have not been implemented or run.
Measured A+B combinations remain blocked until a four-way adapter exists.
decision: Use Agent Eval now for Codex-local, evidence-labeled evaluations; do not treat this self-run as cross-harness outcome proof.
removalPlan: Remove agent-eval@personal and the ~/plugins/agent-eval symlink; canonical JSON remains in this repository.

provenance.harness: Codex desktop orchestrator with method subagents
provenance.model: gpt-5.4 for Promptfoo Codex app-server smoke
provenance.sandbox: orchestrator full workspace access; Promptfoo agent read-only
provenance.network: provider API enabled; Promptfoo agent network access disabled
